The retrieved statements from both EU AI Act (aiact:art_12/par_1#s1) and GDPR (gdpr:art_5/par_1/pt_e#s2) apply to MigrainePredict's operational logs.
This practice complies with both regulations.
MigrainePredict's operational logs are automatically recorded over the lifetime of the system, which meets the obligation stated in aiact:art_12/par_1#s1.
Since the logs are anonymised and cannot be linked to any person, they do not constitute personal data.
Therefore, there is no conflict with gdpr:art_5/par_1/pt_e#s2.

There is insufficient information in the scenario facts to determine whether the data processed by MigrainePredict falls under 'special categories of personal data' as defined in GDPR Article 9(1).

The exception provided in GDPR Article 5(1)(e), which allows for longer storage periods if appropriate technical and organisational measures are implemented, applies here as the logs are anonymised at the point of writing (gdpr:art_5/par_1/pt_e#s1).
Therefore, MigrainePredict's logging practice complies with both regulations.
